category_title: Child with ASD, ADHD, and Specific Learning Disability exhibiting academic resistance and behavioral outbursts
minimal_context: Sam is sitting at his desk in the general education kindergarten classroom with a teacher for a paper and pencil drawing task.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Sam, here is your paper and pencil. Let's practice drawing and coloring.

STUDENT: (Refuses to participate in the lesson and pushes the paper away.) No.

TUTOR: We need to finish this paper and pencil task.

STUDENT: (Gets out of his chair at the desk.)

TUTOR: Please come back to your desk and pick up your pencil.

STUDENT: (Displays rage-like behaviors and begins roaming the classroom.)

options.A: Team academic instruction with the added support of a qualified ABA therapist.
options.B: Model complete sentences as often as possible during the drawing and coloring task.
options.C: Complete consulting notes on a daily basis to share information and strategies.
options.D: Provide paper and pencil worksheets with dotted letters on them for the student.
